author: @murraymadness4674
text:
I'm working on a e-scooter made from a kids bike with 16in wheels.  I don't think you need to mess with a new frame at all.
I just removed the chain, crank and pedals and seat, and put in flip down foot steps.  My hangup is making the longer stem for the handle bars easily removable or folding, so it loads quickly into the backseat of a car.  Everything is been scrap free, so can't buy anything, and my mig welder was stolen.  Usually I'd just weld some stuff together, but more difficult now.   I'd appreciate any ideas on the handle bars, no luck searching google or youtube!  Just shows me commercial folding bikes.

author: @nickclark18
text:
i followed his instructions TO THE LETTER even doing my own deep research and having friends and family helping me, as well as going to the local bike shop and asking thier opinions on certain things and having the expert double check everything to make sure it was good to go. I did EVERYTHING you told me to do, double checked, triple checked... there was nothing else I could have done other than just trust you that you knew what you were talking about..
 MY FIRST RIDE on the bike, and the thing just shreds up on me, the tires went flat, the chain fell off, i crashed... into oncoming traffic and two people almost died... i should sue you for making this video... you didn't tell us that this could be very dangerous... no warning at all..

author: @nicksgarage8295
text: realize with uh structural stength, if you put the angle iron for the parrell pieces instead of triangle up, but L up, it will be stronger cause strength is proportional to height cubed and well, with triangle up the height is only a 45 45 90 or square root of 2 over 2

author: @charlesdickens6706
text: ...yep , nice work. I've got something along similar lines 95% completed in the shed since 1998 . For my build I preferred to bronze braze rather than weld. I added a small seat  and retained the pedal drive also . So mine could be scooted or pedalled . It remains to be seen if I finish the build after a 22 year hiatus . I have a dozen unfinished projects . I should send a pic to your email perhaps .

author: @jamescunliffe9872
text: I would add three long triangular gussets,two at the bottom of the downtbe welded to the bracing rising to the head tube on the underside and one from the top of the head tube to the bracing,side on it should look like a parralelogram. The one tube as it is will break either at the bracing or head tube joint,think stress riser point and weld area + tube diameter? I've had thousands of hours welding cars, bikes,off road parts, test by hitting with a hammer! Looks good when is the race scheduled?:)

author: @amp9438
text: It's looking really great so far. I initially thought as a kick scooter it's always better to have the floorplate as low to the ground as possible to avoid bending down to push. But as an e-scooter it should be great and less of an issue. If anything having it higher prevents the footplate from scrapping/hitting things on the ground and damaging the electronics/batteries etc.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Yes indeed, it's not like I'm going to take it off-road, but doing my morning commute there is a few hills, curbs, and pot holes so I did not want the bottom to hit anything. Especially when some of the electrics are on the bottom of the scooter. Thanks mate! :)
